papers.title: GPT-4 Technical Report
papers.summary: Establishes GPT-4's capabilities, safety evaluations, and the 'predictable scaling' methodology used to forecast performance before training.

insurance_business_case.headline: The baseline 'frontier model' against which every insurance AI build/buy/partner decision should be benchmarked.
points: Sets the performance ceiling for general-purpose copilots in underwriting, claims, and contact-centre.
Provides safety / red-team artifacts the CISO and Model Risk Management teams can reference directly.
Use the predictable-scaling section to defend GPU and budget asks: capability is a forecastable function of compute.

technical_details.pattern: Frontier LLM as a service via Azure OpenAI, with enterprise controls layered on top.
points: Azure OpenAI deployment in Canadian / regional region for data residency.
Private endpoints + VNet + customer-managed keys; no data used for OpenAI training.
Centralized prompt/response logging into the enterprise SIEM for audit.
Model evaluation harness: insurance-specific golden sets gated on every model version upgrade.

papers.title: GPT-4o System Card
papers.summary: Single end-to-end model that natively handles text, audio, and vision in real time at half the cost of GPT-4 Turbo.

insurance_business_case.headline: Unlocks naturally voice-driven insurance experiences — beneficiary updates, claims FNOL, policy explainers — at price points that scale.
points: Voice-first FNOL: customer calls, describes the loss, model captures structured FNOL and confirms coverage within minutes.
Visual claims intake: customer shares photos/video of damage; model converses and asks the right follow-ups.
Multilingual without separate models — material for Manulife Asia, US Hispanic, and Quebec French markets.

technical_details.pattern: Realtime audio LLM behind WebRTC / SIP, with structured-extraction post-processing.
points: Realtime API over WebSockets; voice-activity-detection on the edge for latency.
Function-calling pulls policy data, validates coverage, writes structured FNOL to claims system.
PCI / HIPAA-equivalent posture: payment / PHI redacted on-the-fly via secondary model.
Recording + transcription stored encrypted; consent + retention per provincial regulation.

papers.title: OpenAI o1: Learning to Reason with LLMs
papers.summary: RL-trained model that 'thinks before it speaks' using long internal chains-of-thought, yielding step-changes on math, science, and code benchmarks.

insurance_business_case.headline: Game-changer for the small set of high-value, high-complexity insurance decisions where being right beats being fast.
points: Complex group / large-case underwriting where one decision is worth $millions in premium.
Reinsurance treaty optimization and capital-modelling assistance.
Litigation strategy on complex bad-faith claims — model can reason through legal precedent.

technical_details.pattern: Reasoning-model tier in a multi-tier inference stack, gated by case value / complexity.
points: Router escalates to o-series only when (case value > X) AND (router confidence < threshold).
Budget the 'thinking tokens' per case; alert on outliers for cost control.
Capture and store the reasoning trace separately from the answer for legal privilege management.
Evaluation: track decision quality vs reasoning-token-spend curve quarterly.

papers.title: Sora: Creating Video from Text
papers.summary: Diffusion transformer that generates coherent multi-second video from a text prompt, demonstrating world-model-like physical consistency.

insurance_business_case.headline: Opportunity for radically cheaper customer education and producer training; threat to the integrity of video-evidence in claims.
points: Personalized 30-second policy explainers in the customer's language at issuance.
Producer scenario training (objection handling, suitability reviews) without studio cost.
Risk: video evidence of damage / accidents is no longer self-authenticating — provenance controls become essential.

technical_details.pattern: Generative content pipeline with provenance + a parallel evidence-verification pipeline on the claims side.
points: Creative side: prompt templates + brand-safety filter + legal review workflow before publishing.
Evidence side: C2PA verification on every submitted media asset; cross-check EXIF, geolocation, weather.
Detector model (deepfake/synthetic) as a second-opinion signal; never the sole control.
Track synthetic-media fraud KPIs as a board-level metric.

papers.title: Weak-to-Strong Generalization
papers.summary: Studies whether weaker models / humans can effectively supervise stronger models — a stand-in for the future problem of aligning superhuman AI.

insurance_business_case.headline: Directly informs how Model Risk Management can validate AI systems whose answers their own validators don't fully understand.
points: Designs validation processes for AI used in actuarial work where the model's reasoning may exceed any single validator's expertise.
Supports the case for ensemble + tournament-style validation in MRM (SR 11-7 / OSFI E-23 contexts).
Frames a multi-year capability investment in AI evaluation tooling, not just model tooling.

technical_details.pattern: Ensemble validation: multiple weaker validators (rules, classical ML, junior LLM) cross-checking a frontier model.
points: Validator stack: deterministic rule checks → classical ML benchmark → smaller LLM critic → human spot check.
Disagreement-driven sampling: when validators disagree with the frontier model, escalate.
Maintain a 'capability ledger' tracking what the validators can/cannot reliably check.
Tie model release gates to validator coverage of the model's claimed capabilities.
